mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, consumer spending power contracts sharply and recession fears dominate market pricing, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains above 7.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer and business demand growth, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, which triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The mechanism loses predictive accuracy during post-shock recovery phases, with 5-day forward returns turning positive in more than 50 percent of contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.5 percent, tighter financial conditions increase discount rates and compress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of instances when the threshold is crossed, or the signal shows no edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to generate a statistically significant positive alpha, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of joint signal activations.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising residential investment and construction activity, which signals near-term economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with housing starts, or the signal produces negative returns in over 50 percent of expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, sustained growth in industrial production indicates resilient manufacturing capacity that supports corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions, with 20-day forward returns turning negative in more than 60 percent of high-production periods.
